title: Reply to an Invitation
summary: Respond to an invitation promptly and clearly, accepting or declining politely.
learnerPromise: You will be able to reply to invitations in a way that is respectful and easy for the host to understand.
whyItMatters: Hosts need to know how many people to plan for. A timely reply shows respect and helps you build reliable relationships.
realLifeUses: Replying to a birthday party invite from a friend
Responding to a family dinner invitation
Answering a wedding or formal event invitation
Confirming attendance at a work meeting or social event
materialsNeeded: A phone, computer, or paper to write a reply
result: A clear and polite response to an invitation sent to the host
youWillLearn: Reply to an invitation within a reasonable time
Accept an invitation with warmth and gratitude
Decline an invitation politely without over-explaining
Include key details like who else is coming if asked
miniChallenge: Respond to one real or imaginary invitation. Write a short reply that clearly says whether you are coming and includes a thank-you.
steps: Read the invitation carefully and note the date and details
Decide if you can attend or not
Write a short reply that says yes or no clearly
Add a line of thanks and any needed details
Send your reply to the host
completionCriteria: Your reply clearly states whether you are attending
The message is polite and includes a thank-you
You sent it within a day of receiving the invitation
commonProblems: Waiting too long to reply, which makes planning harder for the host
Giving a vague answer like 'maybe' without a follow-up plan
tips: Reply as soon as you know your answer, even if the deadline is far away
A simple 'Thank you for inviting me, I would love to come' is enough for an acceptance
For a decline, a brief 'Thank you for thinking of me, but I cannot make it' is sufficient
